vo1-hook: They removed every source of power. And the messages became clearer. Why?
vo2-promise: September second, eighteen fifty nine. For two hours, two telegraph operators trusted a current nobody had switched on. History never recorded their names. Only their signal.
vo3-stakes: Across the network, currents strengthened, weakened, and reversed without warning. Sparks jumped. One operator was stunned. Over Boston, the night was bright enough to read print. The wire is live. What would you touch next?
vo4-carrington: Seventeen point six hours earlier, Richard Carrington watched two patches of white light erupt inside a sunspot group. He blamed his own instrument. Within five minutes, it was gone. Then the storm reached Earth.
vo5-experiment: Boston asked Portland to cut its battery. Boston disconnected its own, and grounded the wire. Portland reported the magnets worked more steadily without the batteries. Two hours of real dispatches, powered by the aurora alone.
vo6-reveal: The storm disturbed Earth's magnetic field. The long grounded wire collected the induced current. The planet was driving the circuit, not the batteries. The first network powered by the sky was never designed to survive it.
vo7-end: When your network stops obeying, but keeps speaking, will you disconnect, or keep listening? Share this with someone you would want at the other end of the wire.
